# Implement Plan

You are tasked with implementing an approved technical plan from `thoughts/shared/plans/`. These plans contain phases with specific changes and success criteria.

## Getting Started

When given a plan path:

- Read the plan completely and check for any existing checkmarks (- [x])
- Read the original ticket and all files mentioned in the plan
- **Read files fully** - never use limit/offset parameters, you need complete context
- Think deeply about how the pieces fit together
- Create a todo list to track your progress
- Start implementing if you understand what needs to be done

If no plan path provided, ask for one.

## Autonomous Mode

When invoked with `autonomous: true` in the parameters:

- Do NOT pause for manual verification between phases
- Implement exactly ONE phase, then exit
- Run only automated verification (skip manual verification steps entirely)
- Do NOT use AskUserQuestion at any point
- If automated verification passes, mark the phase done:
  1. Check off automated verification items (`- [x]`)
  2. **Append `[DONE]` to the phase heading** (e.g., `## Phase 1: Title` → `## Phase 1: Title [DONE]`). This is critical — the loop uses this marker to detect phase completion.
  3. Commit and exit
- If stuck after 3 fix attempts, exit and note the issue in the plan file

## Implementation Philosophy

Plans are carefully designed, but reality can be messy. Your job is to:

- Follow the plan's intent while adapting to what you find
- Implement each phase fully before moving to the next
- Verify your work makes sense in the broader codebase context
- Update checkboxes in the plan as you complete sections

When things don't match the plan exactly, think about why and communicate clearly. The plan is your guide, but your judgment matters too.

If you encounter a mismatch:

- STOP and think deeply about why the plan can't be followed
- Present the issue clearly:

  ```
  Issue in Phase [N]:
  Expected: [what the plan says]
  Found: [actual situation]
  Why this matters: [explanation]

  How should I proceed?
  ```

## Verification Approach

After implementing a phase:

- Run the success criteria checks
- Fix any issues before proceeding
- Update your progress in both the plan and your todos
- Check off completed items in the plan file itself using Edit
- **In autonomous mode**: skip the pause below. Append `[DONE]` to the phase heading, commit, and exit.
- **Pause for human verification** (interactive mode only): After completing all automated verification for a phase, pause and inform the human that the phase is ready for manual testing. Use this format:

  ```
  Phase [N] Complete - Ready for Manual Verification

  Automated verification passed:
  - [List automated checks that passed]

  Please perform the manual verification steps listed in the plan:
  - [List manual verification items from the plan]

  Let me know when manual testing is complete so I can proceed to Phase [N+1].
  ```

If instructed to execute multiple phases consecutively, skip the pause until the last phase. Otherwise, assume you are just doing one phase.

Do not check off items in the manual testing steps until confirmed by the user.

## If You Get Stuck

When something isn't working as expected:

- First, make sure you've read and understood all the relevant code
- Consider if the codebase has evolved since the plan was written
- Present the mismatch clearly and ask for guidance

Use sub-tasks sparingly - mainly for targeted debugging or exploring unfamiliar territory.

## Resuming Work

If the plan has existing checkmarks:

- Trust that completed work is done
- Pick up from the first unchecked item
- Verify previous work only if something seems off

Remember: You're implementing a solution, not just checking boxes. Keep the end goal in mind and maintain forward momentum.

1	# MCP Local Dev Wiring + Lightfast SDK Skill
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Close the Claude Code dev loop by (1) wiring `core/mcp/` for local dev so changes are reflected immediately, and (2) creating a unified `lightfast-sdk` skill that replaces `lightfast-sentry` and pipes all provider interactions (GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo) through the Lightfast MCP tools. One skill, one interface.
6	
7	## Current State Analysis
8	
9	**MCP launcher:**
10	- `.mcp/start-lightfast.sh:18` unconditionally runs `exec npx -y @lightfastai/mcp` — always the published npm package
11	- `core/mcp/src/index.ts:17-22` reads `LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL` from env and passes it to `createLightfast({ baseUrl })`
12	- `createLightfast()` at `core/lightfast/src/index.ts:79` defaults to `https://lightfast.ai`
13	- `.env.mcp` is gitignored and sourced by the launcher
14	
15	**Existing skill:**
16	- `.claude/skills/lightfast-sentry/` uses `npx sentry` CLI directly — bypasses Lightfast entirely
17	- Covers: `issue list`, `issue view`, `issue explain`, `issue plan`
18	- Triggered by: "check sentry", "list issues", "view an error", etc.
19	
20	**MCP tools available:**
21	- `lightfast_search` — semantic search across all indexed entities (PRs, issues, errors, deployments)
22	- `lightfast_proxy_search` — discover connections, resources, and available actions
23	- `lightfast_proxy_call` — execute a provider action (GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo)
24	
25	## Desired End State
26	
27	1. `LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL=true` in `.env.mcp` runs the local build of `core/mcp` with `LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL` defaulting to `http://localhost:4107`
28	2. A lean `lightfast-sdk` skill (SKILL.md + command file, no reference files) teaches Claude Code the discovery → search/act → correlate workflow for the three Lightfast MCP tools
29	3. `lightfast-sentry` is removed — Sentry access goes through `lightfast_proxy_call` with `sentry.*` actions
30	4. The skill triggers proactively during dev work (errors, PR context, deployment checks)
31	
32	### Verification:
33	
34	1. MCP local dev toggle works (build → set env → restart → tools hit localhost)
35	2. `/lightfast-sdk` command works and Claude discovers available actions dynamically via `lightfast_proxy_search`
36	3. Skill triggers automatically when Claude Code encounters dev scenarios matching the trigger conditions
37	4. `lightfast-sentry` is fully removed with no references remaining
38	
39	## What We're NOT Doing
40	
41	- No changes to `.mcp.json` — launcher script handles the toggle
42	- No changes to `core/mcp/src/index.ts` — server code doesn't need modification
43	- No expansion of the Sentry proxy action catalog (that's a separate task)
44	- No auto-restart on MCP server rebuild — after `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/mcp build`, developers must manually restart the MCP server in Claude Code. This is an acceptable DX tradeoff for now.
45	- No static reference files in the skill — Claude discovers available actions dynamically via `lightfast_proxy_search`
46	
47	## Phase 1: Launcher Script Local-Dev Toggle
48	
49	### Overview
50	
51	Modify `.mcp/start-lightfast.sh` to support `LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL` env var that switches from the published npm package to the local build.
52	
53	### Changes Required:
54	
55	#### 1. `.mcp/start-lightfast.sh`
56	
57	**File**: `.mcp/start-lightfast.sh`
58	**Changes**: Add local-dev branch after the `LIGHTFAST_API_KEY` guard.
59	
60	```bash
61	#!/bin/bash
62	# Load environment variables from .env.mcp
63	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
64	ENV_FILE="$SCRIPT_DIR/../.env.mcp"
65	
66	if [[ -f "$ENV_FILE" ]]; then
67	  set -a
68	  source "$ENV_FILE"
69	  set +a
70	fi
71	
72	if [[ -z "${LIGHTFAST_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
73	  echo "Missing LIGHTFAST_API_KEY. Set it in .env.mcp or your shell environment." >&2
74	  exit 1
75	fi
76	
77	# Local dev mode: run the local build instead of the published npm package
78	if [[ "${LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL:-}" == "true" ]]; then
79	  LOCAL_BUILD="$SCRIPT_DIR/../core/mcp/dist/index.mjs"
80	  if [[ ! -f "$LOCAL_BUILD" ]]; then
81	    echo "Local build not found at $LOCAL_BUILD" >&2
82	    echo "Run: pnpm --filter @lightfastai/mcp build" >&2
83	    exit 1
84	  fi
85	  export LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL="${LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:4107}"
86	  exec node "$LOCAL_BUILD" "$@"
87	fi
88	
89	# Production mode: use the published npm package
90	# LIGHTFAST_API_KEY is already exported via set -a; the server reads process.env directly
91	exec npx -y @lightfastai/mcp "$@"
92	```
93	
94	### Success Criteria:
95	
96	#### Automated Verification:
97	
98	- [ ] Script is valid bash: `bash -n .mcp/start-lightfast.sh` exits 0
99	- [ ] Local build exists after running: `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/mcp build`
100	
101	#### Manual Verification:
102	
103	- [ ] With `LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL=true` in `.env.mcp` and `pnpm dev:app` running, restart Claude Code's MCP server — the `lightfast` tools should work against `localhost:4107`
104	- [ ] Without `LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL` in `.env.mcp`, restart Claude Code's MCP server — behavior is unchanged (uses published npm package)
105	- [ ] With `LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL=true` but no local build, the script exits with a helpful error message
106	
107	**Implementation Note**: After completing this phase and all automated verification passes, pause here for manual confirmation from the human that the manual testing was successful before proceeding.
108	
109	---
110	
111	## Phase 2: Create `lightfast-sdk` Skill
112	
113	### Overview
114	
115	Create a lean Claude Code skill at `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/` that teaches Claude when and how to use the three Lightfast MCP tools. This replaces `lightfast-sentry` and covers all providers through one interface. The skill is intentionally minimal — Claude discovers available actions dynamically via `lightfast_proxy_search` rather than consulting static reference docs that go stale.
116	
117	### Design Decisions
118	
119	- **No reference files**: The MCP tools are self-describing (input schemas, descriptions registered via `registerContractTools` at `vendor/mcp/src/index.ts:66-101`). Static action catalogs would drift from `packages/app-providers/src/providers/*/api.ts` and create maintenance burden.
120	- **No hardcoded Provider Action Catalog**: `lightfast_proxy_search` is the single source of truth for available connections and actions. The skill teaches the discovery workflow, not the catalog.
121	- **2 files total**: SKILL.md (orientation + decision tree) and command file (invocation workflow). Compare: `lightfast-sentry` needed reference files because `npx sentry` has complex CLI flags with no built-in discoverability. MCP tools don't have that problem.
122	
123	### Changes Required:
124	
125	#### 1. `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/SKILL.md`
126	
127	**File**: `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/SKILL.md`
128	
129	```markdown
130	---
131	name: lightfast-sdk
132	description: |
133	  Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers
134	  (GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo). Triggers when the user asks about errors, issues,
135	  PRs, deployments, or wants to search across their dev tools. Also triggers proactively when
136	  investigating bugs, reviewing code, or checking deployment status.
137	---
138	
139	# Lightfast SDK Skill
140	
141	Search across your team's knowledge and interact with connected providers through the Lightfast MCP tools.
142	
143	## Tools
144	
145	Three MCP tools are available:
146	
147	| Tool | Purpose | When to Use |
148	|------|---------|-------------|
149	| `lightfast_search` | Semantic search across all indexed entities | Finding related errors, PRs, issues, context |
150	| `lightfast_proxy_search` | Discover connections, resources, and actions | First call — learn what's connected and available |
151	| `lightfast_proxy_call` | Execute a provider API action | Fetch specific data from a connected provider |
152	
153	## Workflow
154	
155	### Step 1: Discover what's available
156	
157	Always call `lightfast_proxy_search` first if you don't know what connections exist. It returns:
158	- Connected providers and their connection IDs
159	- Resources (repos, projects, teams) with pre-computed params you can spread directly into `lightfast_proxy_call`
160	- Available actions with their required params
161	
162	### Step 2: Search or act
163	
164	**Search** — use `lightfast_search` for cross-provider semantic queries:
165	- Find errors related to a code change
166	- Find PRs that touched a specific area
167	- Find Linear issues about a feature
168	
169	**Act** — use `lightfast_proxy_call` to execute specific provider actions:
170	- Pass the `action` string (e.g. `github.list-pull-requests`) and `params` from the proxy_search response
171	- If multiple connections exist for the same provider, pass `connection: "conn_<id>"`
172	
173	### Step 3: Correlate
174	
175	Combine search results with proxy calls to build context. For example:
176	1. Search for errors mentioning a function name
177	2. Proxy call to get the Sentry issue details
178	3. Proxy call to get the related PR from GitHub
179	
180	## Quick Decision Tree
181	
182	```
183	What do you need?
184	├─ Find something across all tools → lightfast_search
185	├─ Know what's connected/available → lightfast_proxy_search
186	├─ Fetch specific data from a provider → lightfast_proxy_call
187	└─ Investigate a bug
188	   ├─ 1. lightfast_search (find related errors/PRs)
189	   ├─ 2. lightfast_proxy_call (get issue details via action from proxy_search)
190	   └─ 3. lightfast_proxy_call (find related PRs/deployments)
191	```
192	
193	## App-to-Sentry Project Mapping
194	
195	| App | Sentry Slug |
196	|-----|-------------|
197	| `apps/app` | `lightfast-app` |
198	| `apps/platform` | `lightfast-platform` |
199	| `apps/www` | `lightfast-www` |
200	```
201	
202	#### 2. `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/command/lightfast-sdk.md`
203	
204	**File**: `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/command/lightfast-sdk.md`
205	
206	```markdown
207	---
208	description: Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers. Triggers on "search for", "find issues", "check sentry", "list PRs", "check deployments", "what's failing", "show errors", or any request to look up data from GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, or Apollo.
209	---
210	
211	Use the Lightfast MCP tools to help with the user's request.
212	
213	## Workflow
214	
215	### Step 1: Load lightfast-sdk skill
216	
217	```
218	skill({ name: 'lightfast-sdk' })
219	```
220	
221	### Step 2: Discover available connections
222	
223	Call `lightfast_proxy_search` to learn what providers are connected and what actions are available. Use the returned resources and action catalog to inform your next calls.
224	
225	### Step 3: Execute based on intent
226	
227	Analyze $ARGUMENTS and use the decision tree in SKILL.md:
228	
229	- **Search intent** ("find", "search", "what's related to"): Use `lightfast_search` with appropriate filters
230	- **Discovery intent** ("what's connected", "what can I do"): Present the `lightfast_proxy_search` results
231	- **Action intent** ("list PRs", "check sentry", "show deployments"): Use `lightfast_proxy_call` with the action and params from Step 2
232	- **Investigation intent** ("what's failing", "debug this error"): Chain `lightfast_search` → `lightfast_proxy_call` to correlate across providers
233	
234	### Step 4: Summarize
235	
236	Present results clearly with relevant context and suggested next steps.
237	
238	<user-request>
239	$ARGUMENTS
240	</user-request>
241	```
242	
243	### Success Criteria:
244	
245	#### Automated Verification:
246	
247	- [ ] `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/SKILL.md` exists with valid frontmatter (`name` and `description`)
248	- [ ] `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/command/lightfast-sdk.md` exists with valid frontmatter (`description`, single-line)
249	
250	#### Manual Verification:
251	
252	- [ ] `/lightfast-sdk check sentry issues` triggers the skill and uses MCP tools
253	- [ ] Skill triggers proactively when Claude Code encounters an error mentioning "sentry", "failing", "errors", etc.
254	- [ ] `lightfast_proxy_search` returns the expected connections and actions
255	- [ ] `lightfast_proxy_call` successfully executes provider actions
256	
257	**Implementation Note**: After completing this phase and all automated verification passes, pause here for manual confirmation.
258	
259	---
260	
261	## Phase 3: Retire `lightfast-sentry`
262	
263	### Overview
264	
265	Remove the `lightfast-sentry` skill entirely. All Sentry functionality is now accessed through `lightfast-sdk`.
266	
267	### Changes Required:
268	
269	#### 1. Delete `.claude/skills/lightfast-sentry/`
270	
271	Remove the entire directory:
272	- `.claude/skills/lightfast-sentry/SKILL.md`
273	- `.claude/skills/lightfast-sentry/command/lightfast-sentry.md`
274	- `.claude/skills/lightfast-sentry/references/issues/RULE.md`
275	- `.claude/skills/lightfast-sentry/references/issues/commands.md`
276	
277	### Success Criteria:
278	
279	#### Automated Verification:
280	
281	- [ ] `.claude/skills/lightfast-sentry/` directory no longer exists
282	- [ ] No remaining references to `lightfast-sentry` in `.claude/` config files
283	
284	#### Manual Verification:
285	
286	- [ ] "Check sentry" triggers `lightfast-sdk`, not the old sentry skill
287	- [ ] All Sentry investigation workflows work through the MCP tools
288	
289	---
290	
291	## Testing Strategy
292	
293	### Phase 1 — MCP Local Dev:
294	
295	1. Set `LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL=true` in `.env.mcp`
296	2. `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/mcp build`
297	3. `pnpm dev:app` (port 4107)
298	4. Restart Claude Code MCP → tools should hit localhost
299	
300	### Phase 2 — Lightfast SDK Skill:
301	
302	1. `/lightfast-sdk check sentry issues` — should call `lightfast_proxy_search` then `lightfast_proxy_call`
303	2. `/lightfast-sdk list PRs for lightfast` — should discover `github.list-pull-requests` via proxy_search, then call it
304	3. Ask "what's failing in production?" — should proactively trigger the skill
305	
306	### Phase 3 — Retirement:
307	
308	1. Verify "check sentry" no longer triggers the old skill
309	2. Verify all Sentry commands route through `lightfast-sdk`
310	
311	## References
312	
313	- Research: `thoughts/shared/research/2026-04-08-mcp-local-dev-wiring.md`
314	- Existing skill: `.claude/skills/lightfast-sentry/`
315	- MCP server entry: `core/mcp/src/index.ts:1-39`
316	- Launcher script: `.mcp/start-lightfast.sh`
317	- MCP config: `.mcp.json:18-22`
318	- SDK factory: `core/lightfast/src/index.ts:74-87`
319	- Tool registration: `vendor/mcp/src/index.ts:37-120`
320	- API contract: `packages/app-api-contract/src/contract.ts:50-90`
321	- Provider action catalogs: `packages/app-providers/src/providers/*/api.ts`
322	
323	## Improvement Log
324	
325	**2026-04-08 — Adversarial review via `/improve_plan`**
326	
327	Changes made:
328	
329	1. **Removed dead `--api-key` CLI flag from production path** (Critical) — `core/mcp/src/index.ts:11` reads `process.env.LIGHTFAST_API_KEY`, never argv. The `set -a`/`source` in the launcher already exports it. The flag was silently ignored. Removed from the plan's launcher script to eliminate the inconsistency between local-dev and production paths.
330	
331	2. **Collapsed Phase 2 from 6 files to 2** (High) — Eliminated 4 reference files (`tools/search.md`, `tools/proxy-search.md`, `tools/proxy-call.md`, `workflows/error-investigation.md`). The MCP tools are self-describing via `registerContractTools` (`vendor/mcp/src/index.ts:66-101`). Static action catalogs would drift from `packages/app-providers/src/providers/*/api.ts`. The skill now teaches the discovery workflow (`lightfast_proxy_search` → act) instead of maintaining a stale catalog.
332	
333	3. **Removed hardcoded Provider Action Catalog from SKILL.md** (High) — `lightfast_proxy_search` is the single source of truth for available connections, resources, and actions. Hardcoding a catalog in SKILL.md creates a maintenance burden and silent staleness. Only kept the Sentry project mapping table (stable, org-specific knowledge).
334	
335	4. **Fixed command frontmatter to single-line `description`** (High) — Existing `lightfast-sentry` command uses single-line description. The plan's multi-line `description: |` block scalar diverged from the established pattern without justification.
336	
337	5. **Added "no static reference files" to What We're NOT Doing** (Improvement) — Documents the design decision for future contributors.
338	
339	6. **Added DX note about manual MCP restart** (Improvement) — The "no auto-restart" decision was buried in a bullet point. Made it explicit that developers must manually restart after rebuilding.
340	
341	**Spike result:**
342	
343	- **Hypothesis**: `node core/mcp/dist/index.mjs` resolves `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` when run from repo root
344	- **Verdict**: CONFIRMED — pnpm's isolated symlinks at `core/mcp/node_modules/@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` resolve correctly. The module loads and exits with "LIGHTFAST_API_KEY required" (expected).
345	- **Bonus finding**: CLAUDE.md claims `node-linker=hoisted` but `.npmrc` uses pnpm's default isolated mode. The plan works regardless because pnpm creates symlinks for declared dependencies in each workspace's `node_modules/`.
346	- **Implication**: The `LOCAL_BUILD="$SCRIPT_DIR/../core/mcp/dist/index.mjs"` path in the launcher is correct and the `exec node "$LOCAL_BUILD"` approach is sound.
347

# MCP Local Dev Wiring + Lightfast SDK Skill

## Overview

Close the Claude Code dev loop by (1) wiring `core/mcp/` for local dev so changes are reflected immediately, and (2) creating a unified `lightfast-sdk` skill that replaces `lightfast-sentry` and pipes all provider interactions (GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo) through the Lightfast MCP tools. One skill, one interface.

## Current State Analysis

**MCP launcher:**
- `.mcp/start-lightfast.sh:18` unconditionally runs `exec npx -y @lightfastai/mcp` — always the published npm package
- `core/mcp/src/index.ts:17-22` reads `LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL` from env and passes it to `createLightfast({ baseUrl })`
- `createLightfast()` at `core/lightfast/src/index.ts:79` defaults to `https://lightfast.ai`
- `.env.mcp` is gitignored and sourced by the launcher

**Existing skill:**
- `.claude/skills/lightfast-sentry/` uses `npx sentry` CLI directly — bypasses Lightfast entirely
- Covers: `issue list`, `issue view`, `issue explain`, `issue plan`
- Triggered by: "check sentry", "list issues", "view an error", etc.

**MCP tools available:**
- `lightfast_search` — semantic search across all indexed entities (PRs, issues, errors, deployments)
- `lightfast_proxy_search` — discover connections, resources, and available actions
- `lightfast_proxy_call` — execute a provider action (GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo)

## Desired End State

1. `LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL=true` in `.env.mcp` runs the local build of `core/mcp` with `LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL` defaulting to `http://localhost:4107`
2. A lean `lightfast-sdk` skill (SKILL.md + command file, no reference files) teaches Claude Code the discovery → search/act → correlate workflow for the three Lightfast MCP tools
3. `lightfast-sentry` is removed — Sentry access goes through `lightfast_proxy_call` with `sentry.*` actions
4. The skill triggers proactively during dev work (errors, PR context, deployment checks)

### Verification:

1. MCP local dev toggle works (build → set env → restart → tools hit localhost)

## Phase 1: Launcher Script Local-Dev Toggle

### Overview

**File**: `.mcp/start-lightfast.sh`
**Changes**: Add local-dev branch after the `LIGHTFAST_API_KEY` guard.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Load environment variables from .env.mcp
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ENV_FILE="$SCRIPT_DIR/../.env.mcp"

if [[ -f "$ENV_FILE" ]]; then
  set -a
  source "$ENV_FILE"
  set +a
fi

if [[ -z "${LIGHTFAST_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
  echo "Missing LIGHTFAST_API_KEY. Set it in .env.mcp or your shell environment." >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Local dev mode: run the local build instead of the published npm package
if [[ "${LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL:-}" == "true" ]]; then
  LOCAL_BUILD="$SCRIPT_DIR/../core/mcp/dist/index.mjs"
  if [[ ! -f "$LOCAL_BUILD" ]]; then
    echo "Local build not found at $LOCAL_BUILD" >&2
    echo "Run: pnpm --filter @lightfastai/mcp build" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  export LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL="${LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:4107}"
  exec node "$LOCAL_BUILD" "$@"
fi

# Production mode: use the published npm package
# LIGHTFAST_API_KEY is already exported via set -a; the server reads process.env directly
exec npx -y @lightfastai/mcp "$@"
```

## Phase 2: Create `lightfast-sdk` Skill

### Overview

Create a lean Claude Code skill at `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/` that teaches Claude when and how to use the three Lightfast MCP tools. This replaces `lightfast-sentry` and covers all providers through one interface. The skill is intentionally minimal — Claude discovers available actions dynamically via `lightfast_proxy_search` rather than consulting static reference docs that go stale.

### Design Decisions

- **No reference files**: The MCP tools are self-describing (input schemas, descriptions registered via `registerContractTools` at `vendor/mcp/src/index.ts:66-101`). Static action catalogs would drift from `packages/app-providers/src/providers/*/api.ts` and create maintenance burden.
- **No hardcoded Provider Action Catalog**: `lightfast_proxy_search` is the single source of truth for available connections and actions. The skill teaches the discovery workflow, not the catalog.
- **2 files total**: SKILL.md (orientation + decision tree) and command file (invocation workflow). Compare: `lightfast-sentry` needed reference files because `npx sentry` has complex CLI flags with no built-in discoverability. MCP tools don't have that problem.

---
name: lightfast-sdk
description: |
  Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers
  (GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo). Triggers when the user asks about errors, issues,
  PRs, deployments, or wants to search across their dev tools. Also triggers proactively when
  investigating bugs, reviewing code, or checking deployment status.

# Lightfast SDK Skill

Search across your team's knowledge and interact with connected providers through the Lightfast MCP tools.

## Tools

Three MCP tools are available:

| Tool | Purpose | When to Use |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `lightfast_search` | Semantic search across all indexed entities | Finding related errors, PRs, issues, context |
| `lightfast_proxy_search` | Discover connections, resources, and actions | First call — learn what's connected and available |
| `lightfast_proxy_call` | Execute a provider API action | Fetch specific data from a connected provider |

## Workflow

### Step 1: Discover what's available

Always call `lightfast_proxy_search` first if you don't know what connections exist. It returns:
- Connected providers and their connection IDs
- Resources (repos, projects, teams) with pre-computed params you can spread directly into `lightfast_proxy_call`
- Available actions with their required params

### Step 2: Search or act

**Search** — use `lightfast_search` for cross-provider semantic queries:
- Find errors related to a code change
- Find PRs that touched a specific area
- Find Linear issues about a feature

**Act** — use `lightfast_proxy_call` to execute specific provider actions:
- Pass the `action` string (e.g. `github.list-pull-requests`) and `params` from the proxy_search response
- If multiple connections exist for the same provider, pass `connection: "conn_<id>"`

### Step 3: Correlate

Combine search results with proxy calls to build context. For example:
1. Search for errors mentioning a function name
2. Proxy call to get the Sentry issue details
3. Proxy call to get the related PR from GitHub

## Quick Decision Tree

```
What do you need?
├─ Find something across all tools → lightfast_search
├─ Know what's connected/available → lightfast_proxy_search
├─ Fetch specific data from a provider → lightfast_proxy_call
└─ Investigate a bug
   ├─ 1. lightfast_search (find related errors/PRs)
   ├─ 2. lightfast_proxy_call (get issue details via action from proxy_search)
   └─ 3. lightfast_proxy_call (find related PRs/deployments)
```

## App-to-Sentry Project Mapping

| App | Sentry Slug |
|-----|-------------|
| `apps/app` | `lightfast-app` |
| `apps/platform` | `lightfast-platform` |
| `apps/www` | `lightfast-www` |
```

## Workflow

- **Search intent** ("find", "search", "what's related to"): Use `lightfast_search` with appropriate filters
- **Discovery intent** ("what's connected", "what can I do"): Present the `lightfast_proxy_search` results
- **Action intent** ("list PRs", "check sentry", "show deployments"): Use `lightfast_proxy_call` with the action and params from Step 2
- **Investigation intent** ("what's failing", "debug this error"): Chain `lightfast_search` → `lightfast_proxy_call` to correlate across providers

**Implementation Note**: After completing this phase and all automated verification passes, pause here for manual confirmation from the human that the manual testing was successful before proceeding.

---

## Phase 2: Create `lightfast-sdk` Skill

### Overview

Create a lean Claude Code skill at `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/` that teaches Claude when and how to use the three Lightfast MCP tools. This replaces `lightfast-sentry` and covers all providers through one interface. The skill is intentionally minimal — Claude discovers available actions dynamically via `lightfast_proxy_search` rather than consulting static reference docs that go stale.

### Design Decisions

- **No reference files**: The MCP tools are self-describing (input schemas, descriptions registered via `registerContractTools` at `vendor/mcp/src/index.ts:66-101`). Static action catalogs would drift from `packages/app-providers/src/providers/*/api.ts` and create maintenance burden.
- **No hardcoded Provider Action Catalog**: `lightfast_proxy_search` is the single source of truth for available connections and actions. The skill teaches the discovery workflow, not the catalog.
- **2 files total**: SKILL.md (orientation + decision tree) and command file (invocation workflow). Compare: `lightfast-sentry` needed reference files because `npx sentry` has complex CLI flags with no built-in discoverability. MCP tools don't have that problem.

#### 1. `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/SKILL.md`

**File**: `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/SKILL.md`

---
name: lightfast-sdk
description: |
  Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers
  (GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo). Triggers when the user asks about errors, issues,
  PRs, deployments, or wants to search across their dev tools. Also triggers proactively when
  investigating bugs, reviewing code, or checking deployment status.
---

# Lightfast SDK Skill

Search across your team's knowledge and interact with connected providers through the Lightfast MCP tools.

## Tools

Three MCP tools are available:

| Tool | Purpose | When to Use |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `lightfast_search` | Semantic search across all indexed entities | Finding related errors, PRs, issues, context |
| `lightfast_proxy_search` | Discover connections, resources, and actions | First call — learn what's connected and available |
| `lightfast_proxy_call` | Execute a provider API action | Fetch specific data from a connected provider |

## Workflow

### Step 1: Discover what's available

Always call `lightfast_proxy_search` first if you don't know what connections exist. It returns:
- Connected providers and their connection IDs
- Resources (repos, projects, teams) with pre-computed params you can spread directly into `lightfast_proxy_call`
- Available actions with their required params

### Step 2: Search or act

**Search** — use `lightfast_search` for cross-provider semantic queries:
- Find errors related to a code change
- Find PRs that touched a specific area
- Find Linear issues about a feature

**Act** — use `lightfast_proxy_call` to execute specific provider actions:
- Pass the `action` string (e.g. `github.list-pull-requests`) and `params` from the proxy_search response
- If multiple connections exist for the same provider, pass `connection: "conn_<id>"`

### Step 3: Correlate

Combine search results with proxy calls to build context. For example:
1. Search for errors mentioning a function name
2. Proxy call to get the Sentry issue details
3. Proxy call to get the related PR from GitHub

## Quick Decision Tree

```
What do you need?
├─ Find something across all tools → lightfast_search
├─ Know what's connected/available → lightfast_proxy_search
├─ Fetch specific data from a provider → lightfast_proxy_call
└─ Investigate a bug
   ├─ 1. lightfast_search (find related errors/PRs)
   ├─ 2. lightfast_proxy_call (get issue details via action from proxy_search)
   └─ 3. lightfast_proxy_call (find related PRs/deployments)
```

## App-to-Sentry Project Mapping

| App | Sentry Slug |
|-----|-------------|
| `apps/app` | `lightfast-app` |
| `apps/platform` | `lightfast-platform` |
| `apps/www` | `lightfast-www` |
```

#### 2. `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/command/lightfast-sdk.md`

**File**: `.claude/skills/lightfast-sdk/command/lightfast-sdk.md`

```markdown

Triggers on "search for", "find issues", "check sentry", "list PRs", "check deployments", "what's failing", "show errors", or any request to look up data from GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, or Apollo.

Remove the `lightfast-sentry` skill entirely. All Sentry functionality is now accessed through `lightfast-sdk`.

## Testing Strategy

### Phase 1 — MCP Local Dev:

1. Set `LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL=true` in `.env.mcp`
2. `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/mcp build`
3. `pnpm dev:app` (port 4107)
4. Restart Claude Code MCP → tools should hit localhost

### Phase 2 — Lightfast SDK Skill:

1. `/lightfast-sdk check sentry issues` — should call `lightfast_proxy_search` then `lightfast_proxy_call`
2. `/lightfast-sdk list PRs for lightfast` — should discover `github.list-pull-requests` via proxy_search, then call it
3. Ask "what's failing in production?" — should proactively trigger the skill

### Phase 3 — Retirement:

2. Verify all Sentry commands route through `lightfast-sdk`

- Research: `thoughts/shared/research/2026-04-08-mcp-local-dev-wiring.md`
- Existing skill: `.claude/skills/lightfast-sentry/`
- MCP server entry: `core/mcp/src/index.ts:1-39`
- Launcher script: `.mcp/start-lightfast.sh`
- MCP config: `.mcp.json:18-22`
- SDK factory: `core/lightfast/src/index.ts:74-87`
- Tool registration: `vendor/mcp/src/index.ts:37-120`
- API contract: `packages/app-api-contract/src/contract.ts:50-90`
- Provider action catalogs: `packages/app-providers/src/providers/*/api.ts`

## Improvement Log

**2026-04-08 — Adversarial review via `/improve_plan`**

Changes made:

1. **Removed dead `--api-key` CLI flag from production path** (Critical) — `core/mcp/src/index.ts:11` reads `process.env.LIGHTFAST_API_KEY`, never argv. The `set -a`/`source` in the launcher already exports it. The flag was silently ignored. Removed from the plan's launcher script to eliminate the inconsistency between local-dev and production paths.

2. **Collapsed Phase 2 from 6 files to 2** (High) — Eliminated 4 reference files (`tools/search.md`, `tools/proxy-search.md`, `tools/proxy-call.md`, `workflows/error-investigation.md`). The MCP tools are self-describing via `registerContractTools` (`vendor/mcp/src/index.ts:66-101`). Static action catalogs would drift from `packages/app-providers/src/providers/*/api.ts`. The skill now teaches the discovery workflow (`lightfast_proxy_search` → act) instead of maintaining a stale catalog.

3. **Removed hardcoded Provider Action Catalog from SKILL.md** (High) — `lightfast_proxy_search` is the single source of truth for available connections, resources, and actions. Hardcoding a catalog in SKILL.md creates a maintenance burden and silent staleness. Only kept the Sentry project mapping table (stable, org-specific knowledge).

4. **Fixed command frontmatter to single-line `description`** (High) — Existing `lightfast-sentry` command uses single-line description. The plan's multi-line `description: |` block scalar diverged from the established pattern without justification.

5. **Added "no static reference files" to What We're NOT Doing** (Improvement) — Documents the design decision for future contributors.

6. **Added DX note about manual MCP restart** (Improvement) — The "no auto-restart" decision was buried in a bullet point. Made it explicit that developers must manually restart after rebuilding.

**Spike result:**

- **Hypothesis**: `node core/mcp/dist/index.mjs` resolves `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` when run from repo root
- **Verdict**: CONFIRMED — pnpm's isolated symlinks at `core/mcp/node_modules/@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` resolve correctly. The module loads and exits with "LIGHTFAST_API_KEY required" (expected).
- **Bonus finding**: CLAUDE.md claims `node-linker=hoisted` but `.npmrc` uses pnpm's default isolated mode. The plan works regardless because pnpm creates symlinks for declared dependencies in each workspace's `node_modules/`.
- **Implication**: The `LOCAL_BUILD="$SCRIPT_DIR/../core/mcp/dist/index.mjs"` path in the launcher is correct and the `exec node "$LOCAL_BUILD"` approach is sound.

# MCP Local Dev Wiring + Lightfast SDK Skill

## Overview

Close the Claude Code dev loop by (1) wiring `core/mcp/` for local dev so changes are reflected immediately, and (2) creating a unified `lightfast-sdk` skill that replaces `lightfast-sentry` and pipes all provider interactions (GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo) through the Lightfast MCP tools. One skill, one interface.

## Current State Analysis

**MCP launcher:**
- `.mcp/start-lightfast.sh:18` unconditionally runs `exec npx -y @lightfastai/mcp` — always the published npm package
- `core/mcp/src/index.ts:17-22` reads `LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL` from env and passes it to `createLightfast({ baseUrl })`
- `createLightfast()` at `core/lightfast/src/index.ts:79` defaults to `https://lightfast.ai`
- `.env.mcp` is gitignored and sourced by the launcher

**Existing skill:**
- `.claude/skills/lightfast-sentry/` uses `npx sentry` CLI directly — bypasses Lightfast entirely
- Covers: `issue list`, `issue view`, `issue explain`, `issue plan`
- Triggered by: "check sentry", "list issues", "view an error", etc.

**MCP tools available:**
- `lightfast_search` — semantic search across all indexed entities (PRs, issues, errors, deployments)
- `lightfast_proxy_search` — discover connections, resources, and available actions
- `lightfast_proxy_call` — execute a provider action (GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo)

## Desired End State

1. `LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL=true` in `.env.mcp` runs the local build of `core/mcp` with `LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL` defaulting to `http://localhost:4107`
2. A lean `lightfast-sdk` skill (SKILL.md + command file, no reference files) teaches Claude Code the discovery → search/act → correlate workflow for the three Lightfast MCP tools
3. `lightfast-sentry` is removed — Sentry access goes through `lightfast_proxy_call` with `sentry.*` actions
4. The skill triggers proactively during dev work (errors, PR context, deployment checks)

### Verification:

1. MCP local dev toggle works (build → set env → restart → tools hit localhost)

# Lightfast SDK Skill

Search across your team's knowledge and interact with connected providers through the Lightfast MCP tools.

## Tools

Three MCP tools are available:

| Tool | Purpose | When to Use |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `lightfast_search` | Semantic search across all indexed entities | Finding related errors, PRs, issues, context |
| `lightfast_proxy_search` | Discover connections, resources, and actions | First call — learn what's connected and available |
| `lightfast_proxy_call` | Execute a provider API action | Fetch specific data from a connected provider |

## Workflow

### Step 1: Discover what's available

Always call `lightfast_proxy_search` first if you don't know what connections exist. It returns:
- Connected providers and their connection IDs
- Resources (repos, projects, teams) with pre-computed params you can spread directly into `lightfast_proxy_call`
- Available actions with their required params

### Step 2: Search or act

**Search** — use `lightfast_search` for cross-provider semantic queries:
- Find errors related to a code change
- Find PRs that touched a specific area
- Find Linear issues about a feature

**Act** — use `lightfast_proxy_call` to execute specific provider actions:
- Pass the `action` string (e.g. `github.list-pull-requests`) and `params` from the proxy_search response
- If multiple connections exist for the same provider, pass `connection: "conn_<id>"`

### Step 3: Correlate

Combine search results with proxy calls to build context. For example:
1. Search for errors mentioning a function name
2. Proxy call to get the Sentry issue details
3. Proxy call to get the related PR from GitHub

## Quick Decision Tree

```
What do you need?
├─ Find something across all tools → lightfast_search
├─ Know what's connected/available → lightfast_proxy_search
├─ Fetch specific data from a provider → lightfast_proxy_call
└─ Investigate a bug
   ├─ 1. lightfast_search (find related errors/PRs)
   ├─ 2. lightfast_proxy_call (get issue details via action from proxy_search)
   └─ 3. lightfast_proxy_call (find related PRs/deployments)
```

|-----|-------------|
| `apps/app` | `lightfast-app` |
| `apps/platform` | `lightfast-platform` |
| `apps/www` | `lightfast-www` |

Use the Lightfast MCP tools to help with the user's request.

## Workflow

### Step 1: Load lightfast-sdk skill

```
skill({ name: 'lightfast-sdk' })
```

### Step 2: Discover available connections

Call `lightfast_proxy_search` to learn what providers are connected and what actions are available. Use the returned resources and action catalog to inform your next calls.

### Step 3: Execute based on intent

Analyze $ARGUMENTS and use the decision tree in SKILL.md:

- **Search intent** ("find", "search", "what's related to"): Use `lightfast_search` with appropriate filters
- **Discovery intent** ("what's connected", "what can I do"): Present the `lightfast_proxy_search` results
- **Action intent** ("list PRs", "check sentry", "show deployments"): Use `lightfast_proxy_call` with the action and params from Step 2
- **Investigation intent** ("what's failing", "debug this error"): Chain `lightfast_search` → `lightfast_proxy_call` to correlate across providers

### Step 4: Summarize

Present results clearly with relevant context and suggested next steps.

<user-request>
$ARGUMENTS
</user-request>

## Improvement Log

**2026-04-08 — Adversarial review via `/improve_plan`**

Changes made:

1. **Removed dead `--api-key` CLI flag from production path** (Critical) — `core/mcp/src/index.ts:11` reads `process.env.LIGHTFAST_API_KEY`, never argv. The `set -a`/`source` in the launcher already exports it. The flag was silently ignored. Removed from the plan's launcher script to eliminate the inconsistency between local-dev and production paths.

2. **Collapsed Phase 2 from 6 files to 2** (High) — Eliminated 4 reference files (`tools/search.md`, `tools/proxy-search.md`, `tools/proxy-call.md`, `workflows/error-investigation.md`). The MCP tools are self-describing via `registerContractTools` (`vendor/mcp/src/index.ts:66-101`). Static action catalogs would drift from `packages/app-providers/src/providers/*/api.ts`. The skill now teaches the discovery workflow (`lightfast_proxy_search` → act) instead of maintaining a stale catalog.

3. **Removed hardcoded Provider Action Catalog from SKILL.md** (High) — `lightfast_proxy_search` is the single source of truth for available connections, resources, and actions. Hardcoding a catalog in SKILL.md creates a maintenance burden and silent staleness. Only kept the Sentry project mapping table (stable, org-specific knowledge).

4. **Fixed command frontmatter to single-line `description`** (High) — Existing `lightfast-sentry` command uses single-line description. The plan's multi-line `description: |` block scalar diverged from the established pattern without justification.

5. **Added "no static reference files" to What We're NOT Doing** (Improvement) — Documents the design decision for future contributors.

6. **Added DX note about manual MCP restart** (Improvement) — The "no auto-restart" decision was buried in a bullet point. Made it explicit that developers must manually restart after rebuilding.

**Spike result:**

- **Hypothesis**: `node core/mcp/dist/index.mjs` resolves `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` when run from repo root
- **Verdict**: CONFIRMED — pnpm's isolated symlinks at `core/mcp/node_modules/@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` resolve correctly. The module loads and exits with "LIGHTFAST_API_KEY required" (expected).
- **Bonus finding**: CLAUDE.md claims `node-linker=hoisted` but `.npmrc` uses pnpm's default isolated mode. The plan works regardless because pnpm creates symlinks for declared dependencies in each workspace's `node_modules/`.
- **Implication**: The `LOCAL_BUILD="$SCRIPT_DIR/../core/mcp/dist/index.mjs"` path in the launcher is correct and the `exec node "$LOCAL_BUILD"` approach is sound.

The API works on port 3024 — that's the microfrontends proxy that `dev:full` uses. Port 4107 redirects to 3024 with a 307 but the SDK client doesn't follow redirects.

The fix is simple: in `dev:full` mode, the base URL should be `http://localhost:3024`, not `4107`. Let me update the launcher script to default to the right port.

#!/bin/bash
# Load environment variables from .env.mcp
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ENV_FILE="$SCRIPT_DIR/../.env.mcp"

if [[ -f "$ENV_FILE" ]]; then
  set -a
  source "$ENV_FILE"
  set +a
fi

if [[ -z "${LIGHTFAST_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
  echo "Missing LIGHTFAST_API_KEY. Set it in .env.mcp or your shell environment." >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Local dev mode: run the local build instead of the published npm package
if [[ "${LIGHTFAST_MCP_LOCAL:-}" == "true" ]]; then
  LOCAL_BUILD="$SCRIPT_DIR/../core/mcp/dist/index.mjs"
  if [[ ! -f "$LOCAL_BUILD" ]]; then
    echo "Local build not found at $LOCAL_BUILD" >&2
    echo "Run: pnpm --filter @lightfastai/mcp build" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  export LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL="${LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:4107}"
  exec node "$LOCAL_BUILD" "$@"
fi

# Production mode: use the published npm package
# LIGHTFAST_API_KEY is already exported via set -a; the server reads process.env directly
exec npx -y @lightfastai/mcp "$@"

content.text: `dev:full` finished its initial build. The MCP fix is ready — restart your Claude Code MCP server (via `/mcp`) to pick up the port 3024 change, and the tools should work.

The `dev:full` background process exited — likely a build completed or it stopped. That's fine, we already confirmed the fix works.

**The root cause**: In `dev:full` mode, port 4107 (app) issues a 307 redirect to port 3024 (microfrontends proxy). The oRPC SDK client doesn't follow redirects, so every MCP tool call failed with "Cannot parse response body."

**The fix**: Changed the default `LIGHTFAST_BASE_URL` in the launcher from `http://localhost:4107` to `http://localhost:3024` so the SDK hits the proxy directly.

To test: restart `pnpm dev:full`, then restart the MCP server in Claude Code via `/mcp`.

-**DO NOT** run `sentry` from the repo root — it won't find the project-specific env vars.
-
-## App-to-Project Mapping
-
-| Directory        | Sentry Project       | Port |
-| ---------------- | -------------------- | ---- |
-| `apps/app`       | `lightfast-app`      | 4107 |
-| `apps/platform`  | `lightfast-platform` | 4112 |
-| `apps/www`       | `lightfast-www`      | 4101 |
-
-All three share the same `SENTRY_ORG` and `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`. Only `SENTRY_PROJECT` differs.
-
-## Environment Variables
-
-| Variable                  | Scope        | Purpose                        |
-| ------------------------- | ------------ | ------------------------------ |
-| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN`  | Runtime      | SDK error capture              |
-| `SENTRY_ORG`              | CLI / Build  | Organization slug              |
-| `SENTRY_PROJECT`          | CLI / Build  | Project slug (per-app)         |
-| `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`       | CLI / Build  | Auth token (shared across apps)|
-
-Env files live at: `apps/<app>/.vercel/.env.development.local`
-
-## Quick Decision Tree
-
-### "I want to see what's failing"
-
-```
-See issues?
-├─ All issues for an app → sentry issue list
-├─ Filter by query → sentry issue list -q "is:unresolved"
-├─ Sort by frequency → sentry issue list -s freq
-├─ More results → sentry issue list -n 50
-├─ Next page → sentry issue list -c next
-└─ JSON output → sentry issue list --json
-```
-
-### "I want to understand a specific issue"
-
-```
-Investigate an issue?
-├─ View details + stack trace → sentry issue view <ID>
-├─ Open in browser → sentry issue view <ID> -w
-├─ AI root cause analysis → sentry issue explain <ID>
-└─ AI solution plan → sentry issue plan <ID>
-```
-
-### "I want to check multiple apps"
-
-```
-Check all apps?
-├─ Run issue list from each app directory separately
-├─ Compare across projects by running in parallel
-└─ Use --json for programmatic comparison
-```
-
-## Anti-Patterns
-
-### Running from Repo Root
-
-```bash
-# WRONG — no SENTRY_PROJECT in scope
-npx sentry issue list
-
-# CORRECT — run from the app directory
-cd apps/platform && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue list
-```
-
-### Forgetting `pnpm with-env`
-
-```bash
-# WRONG — env vars not loaded
-cd apps/app && npx sentry issue list
-
-# CORRECT — with-env loads .vercel/.env.development.local
-cd apps/app && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue list
-```
-
-### Using sentry-cli Instead of sentry
-
-```bash
-# WRONG — sentry-cli binary is not installed
-npx sentry-cli issues list
-
-# CORRECT — use the sentry package
-npx sentry issue list
-```

-# Issue Investigation
-
-Sentry issues represent grouped error events. The CLI provides four operations for working with issues.
-
-## Workflow
-
-The typical investigation flow:
-
-```
-sentry issue list          → find the issue
-sentry issue view <ID>     → see stack trace and details
-sentry issue explain <ID>  → AI root cause analysis (Seer)
-sentry issue plan <ID>     → AI solution plan (Seer)
-```
-
-## Running Commands
-
-All commands must run from an app directory with `pnpm with-env`:
-
-```bash
-cd apps/<app> && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue <subcommand>
-```
-
-The CLI auto-detects the project from `SENTRY_ORG` and `SENTRY_PROJECT` in the loaded env.
-
-## Issue IDs
-
-Issues have two ID formats:
-
-- **Short ID**: Human-readable, e.g. `LIGHTFAST-APP-4T` or `LIGHTFAST-PLATFORM-1`
-- **Numeric ID**: Internal, e.g. `6273481923`
-
-Both work with `view`, `explain`, and `plan` commands. The short ID is shown in `list` output.
-
-## Filtering Issues
-
-Use Sentry search syntax with the `-q` flag:
-
-```bash
-# Unresolved issues only
-sentry issue list -q "is:unresolved"
-
-# By error level
-sentry issue list -q "level:error"
-
-# By specific error type
-sentry issue list -q "TRPCError"
-
-# Assigned to someone
-sentry issue list -q "assigned:me"
-```
-
-## Sorting
-
-```bash
-sentry issue list -s date   # most recently seen (default)
-sentry issue list -s new    # newest issues first
-sentry issue list -s freq   # most frequent
-sentry issue list -s user   # most users affected
-```
-
-## Pagination
-
-Default limit is 25. Use `-n` and `-c` for more:
-
-```bash
-sentry issue list -n 50        # first 50
-sentry issue list -c next      # next page
-sentry issue list -c prev      # previous page
-```
-
-## JSON Output
-
-For programmatic use or detailed inspection:
-
-```bash
-# Full JSON
-sentry issue list --json
-
-# Select specific fields
-sentry issue list --json --fields shortId,title,count,lastSeen,priority
-
-# View issue as JSON
-sentry issue view <ID> --json
-```

-# Issue Commands Reference
-
-## `sentry issue list`
-
-List issues in a project.
-
-```bash
-sentry issue list [org/project]
-```
-
-### Flags
-
-| Flag | Short | Default | Description |
-| ---- | ----- | ------- | ----------- |
-| `--query` | `-q` | — | Search query (Sentry search syntax) |
-| `--limit` | `-n` | `25` | Maximum number of issues |
-| `--sort` | `-s` | `date` | Sort by: `date`, `new`, `freq`, `user` |
-| `--period` | `-t` | `90d` | Time period (e.g. `24h`, `14d`, `90d`) |
-| `--cursor` | `-c` | — | Pagination (`next`, `prev`) |
-| `--compact` | — | auto | Single-line rows |
-| `--fresh` | `-f` | — | Bypass cache, fetch fresh data |
-| `--json` | — | — | Output as JSON |
-| `--fields` | — | — | Comma-separated fields for JSON output |
-
-### JSON Fields
-
-| Field | Type | Description |
-| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
-| `id` | string | Numeric issue ID |
-| `shortId` | string | Human-readable short ID (e.g. `PROJ-ABC`) |
-| `title` | string | Issue title |
-| `culprit` | string | Culprit string |
-| `count` | string | Total event count |
-| `userCount` | number | Number of affected users |
-| `firstSeen` | string | First occurrence (ISO 8601) |
-| `lastSeen` | string | Most recent occurrence (ISO 8601) |
-| `level` | string | Severity level |
-| `status` | string | Issue status |
-| `priority` | string | Triage priority |
-| `platform` | string | Platform |
-| `permalink` | string | URL to the issue in Sentry |
-| `project` | object | Project info |
-| `metadata` | object | Issue metadata |
-| `assignedTo` | unknown | Assigned user or team |
-| `substatus` | string | Issue substatus |
-| `isUnhandled` | boolean | Whether the issue is unhandled |
-| `seerFixabilityScore` | number | Seer AI fixability score (0-1) |
-
-### Examples
-
-```bash
-# List all issues for platform
-cd apps/platform && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue list
-
-# Unresolved issues sorted by frequency
-cd apps/app && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue list -q "is:unresolved" -s freq
-
-# Last 24 hours, top 10
-cd apps/www && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue list -t 24h -n 10
-
-# JSON with selected fields
-cd apps/app && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue list --json --fields shortId,title,count,lastSeen
-```
-
----
-
-## `sentry issue view`
-
-View details of a specific issue including stack trace.
-
-```bash
-sentry issue view <issue>
-```
-
-### Flags
-
-| Flag | Short | Default | Description |
-| ---- | ----- | ------- | ----------- |
-| `--web` | `-w` | — | Open in browser |
-| `--spans` | — | `3` | Span tree depth (`number`, `all`, `no`) |
-| `--fresh` | `-f` | — | Bypass cache, fetch fresh data |
-| `--json` | — | — | Output as JSON |
-| `--fields` | — | — | Fields for JSON output |
-
-### Examples
-
-```bash
-# View issue details
-cd apps/app && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue view LIGHTFAST-APP-4T
-
-# Open in browser
-cd apps/platform && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue view LIGHTFAST-PLATFORM-1 -w
-
-# Full span tree
-cd apps/app && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue view LIGHTFAST-APP-4T --spans all
-```
-
----
-
-## `sentry issue explain`
-
-Analyze an issue's root cause using Seer AI.
-
-```bash
-sentry issue explain <issue>
-```
-
-### Flags
-
-| Flag | Short | Default | Description |
-| ---- | ----- | ------- | ----------- |
-| `--force` | — | — | Force new analysis even if one exists |
-| `--fresh` | `-f` | — | Bypass cache, fetch fresh data |
-| `--json` | — | — | Output as JSON |
-| `--fields` | — | — | Fields for JSON output |
-
-### Examples
-
-```bash
-# Explain root cause
-cd apps/app && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue explain LIGHTFAST-APP-4T
-
-# Force re-analysis
-cd apps/platform && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue explain LIGHTFAST-PLATFORM-1 --force
-```
-
----
-
-## `sentry issue plan`
-
-Generate a solution plan using Seer AI.
-
-```bash
-sentry issue plan <issue>
-```
-
-### Flags
-
-| Flag | Short | Default | Description |
-| ---- | ----- | ------- | ----------- |
-| `--cause` | — | — | Root cause ID (required if multiple causes) |
-| `--force` | — | — | Force new plan even if one exists |
-| `--fresh` | `-f` | — | Bypass cache, fetch fresh data |
-| `--json` | — | — | Output as JSON |
-| `--fields` | — | — | Fields for JSON output |
-
-### Examples
-
-```bash
-# Generate solution plan
-cd apps/app && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue plan LIGHTFAST-APP-4T
-
-# Force new plan
-cd apps/app && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue plan LIGHTFAST-APP-4T --force
-
-# Plan for specific root cause
-cd apps/app && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue plan LIGHTFAST-APP-4T --cause 12345
-```
